# Brown2Green

> Turn your brownfield codebase into an AI-ready project.

Brown2Green is a methodology for making legacy codebases work effectively with AI coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Core Concept

Brownfield codebases have implicit conventions, undocumented side effects, and tightly-coupled modules that AI agents can't navigate. Brown2Green provides a structured approach to fix this.

## Six-Phase Methodology

1. **Assess** — Map architecture, identify pain points, establish AI readiness baseline
2. **Document** — Create documentation that makes codebases navigable by AI agents
3. **Guard** — Set up hooks and guardrails to prevent AI agents from breaking things
4. **Specialise** — Build specialised sub-agents with focused skills per domain
5. **Workflow** — Define repeatable workflows with consistent quality pipelines
6. **Iterate** — Continuous improvement, each cycle increases AI readiness

## Specialised Agents

- `@codebase-qa` — Knowledge: answers questions about codebase
- `@impact-analyser` — Safety: predicts what breaks before changes
- `@feature-planner` — Planning: produces file-by-file implementation plans
- `@feature-implementer` — Building: writes files following conventions
- `@preflight` — QA: ESLint + TypeScript + production build gate
- `@reviewer` — Review: reviews diffs, reports by severity
- `@committer` — Git: proposes logical commit breakdowns
- `@documenter` — Docs: generates architecture docs with diagrams

## Workflows

- **Simple** (1–3 files): Understand → Impact check → Change → Preflight → Review → Commit
- **Complex** (multi-layer): Plan → Implement → QA → Review → Commit → Document

## Documentation Structure

```
project/
├── CLAUDE.md          # Agent conventions & rules
├── AGENTS.md          # Agent definitions & skills
├── docs/
│   ├── requirements/  # What needs to be built
│   ├── plans/         # How it will be built
│   ├── features/      # What was built
│   └── manual/        # How everything works
├── .hooks/            # Pre-commit, access controls
└── .agents/           # Agent skill definitions
```

## Links

- Website: https://brown2green.dev
- Author: Jack McNicol (https://jack.mcpickle.com.au)
